file_title: Enhancing Patient Communication for Service Efficiency
introduction: As MediConn Solutions continues to grow, effective patient communication becomes increasingly pivotal in maintaining customer satisfaction and operational efficiency. This presentation explores strategies to enhance our patient communication, aiming to reduce service calls in Q1 2025.

subsections.heading: Understanding Current Patient Communication Channels
subsections.content:
• As of Q3 2024, Ontario patients have been able to utilize provincial telehealth billing codes, leading to a cost reduction of 20%.
• In contrast, British Columbia patients are billed at standard rates, with no provincial codes applicable.
• Consequently, these billing practices impact the information our customer service can provide to patients regarding their financial responsibilities.
• Our customer service team is equipped to communicate administrative follow-up procedures like booking referrals, while strictly adhering to regulations that prohibit the provision of medical advice.
• As we move into 2025, the focus is on expanding our patient communication strategies to keep up with the evolving healthcare landscape.
• We need to explore AI technology as a potential tool for enhancing patient communication, as suggested by Andrew Hadje of MGMA.
• Only 13% of healthcare group leaders are planning to focus on AI in the next 12 months, but we can position ourselves as industry pioneers by adopting it sooner.
subsections.justification: This content outlines existing communication practices and highlights potential areas for improvement, answering the DR question by providing a roadmap for enhancing patient communication.

subsections.heading: Analyzing Service Call Trends and Causes
subsections.content:
• In 2023, the leading cause for service calls was billing inquiries
• We noticed a 20% increase in service calls during flu season
• The average call duration in 2024 was 8 minutes
• Our peak call times are typically between 10 AM and 2 PM
• We have seen a 10% reduction in calls related to appointment scheduling since implementing online booking
• Our call abandonment rate has remained consistent at around 5% over the past two years
• The majority of service calls are made by patients aged 50 and above
subsections.justification: This content focuses on historical trends and reasons for calls but doesn't suggest ways to improve patient communication or reduce calls.

subsections.heading: Proposed Strategies for Improved Patient Communication
subsections.content:
• Implementing an FAQ section on our website to proactively answer common patient queries
• Offering webinar sessions for patients to understand how to navigate our digital platform
• Utilizing social media platforms to provide updates and information
• Increasing the size of our customer service team to handle higher call volumes
• Exploring the possibility of a patient forum for peer-to-peer communication
• Conducting regular customer satisfaction surveys to identify areas of improvement
• Introducing virtual consultation options to reduce the need for physical visits
subsections.justification: This content proposes general communication improvements, but doesn't address how to specifically reduce service calls into Q1 2025.

conclusion: By leveraging our understanding of existing patient communication channels and service call causes, we can effectively implement refined communication strategies. These strategies aim to enhance patient experience and reduce service calls into Q1 2025.
